content: If we map the human language learning process of reinforcing reflection (from the high school English vocabulary teaching context of reflexion) to LLM iterative refinement, we can build a human-like learning framework where LLMs refine their output not just through technical self-feedback, but through explicit metalinguistic reflection on linguistic form, just like human students refine their writing through repeated vocabulary and structure reflection. This framework can address the common problem that current Self-Refine methods fail to consistently improve stylistic and grammatical quality of LLM-generated creative or academic writing.
